title: CryptoJews
description: Population genetics critique of uniparental marker methodology for inferring Jewish ancestry in Iberian crypto-Jewish communities
abstract: This commentary critiques Nogueiro et al. (2015), who used Y chromosome and mitochondrial DNA haplotype data from contemporary Iberian and non-Iberian populations to explore a diagnostic genetic signature of Portuguese "crypto-Jews." The authors identify two main weaknesses: (1) the matrilineal and patrilineal markers reviewed are methodologically inadequate as "reliable Jewish ancestry predictors" due to haplotype polyphyly and molecular clock ambiguity — many ostensibly "Jewish" haplotypes are
